behavior_summary: U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider animates a world "slider" item toward an activated state: it plays an SFX, reads the item's current frame, and either increments the frame (continuing a multi-step animation using spawn/suspend) or, when the frame reaches a terminal value (0x04), maps the item's type to an "activated" counterpart and calls FREE::changeItemType at the item's (Z,Y,X) to replace the world tile.

U8P_SLIDER::deactivateSlider performs the inverse: plays the same SFX, detects the activated world type, maps it back to the original (deactivated) type, calls FREE::changeItemType (with a different flag), and steps the item's frame down toward zero using suspend/spawn to pace the retreat animation. If the type-change fails, the deactivation sequence aborts.

Both routines rely on Item::* accessors to read/set frame and coordinates, use spawn (-> FREE::func1C19) and suspend to produce multi-frame animation over time, and use hard-coded type comparisons to choose the paired type to swap in the world.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider: playSFX -> read Item::getFrame(this) -> if frame == 0x04 then compute target type via Item::getType checks -> call FREE::changeItemType(z,y,x,0x00,targetType,this) else increment frame, Item::setFrame, spawn helper, suspend and continue.

U8P_SLIDER::deactivateSlider: playSFX -> compute deactivated type via Item::getType checks -> read coordinates and call FREE::changeItemType(z,y,x,0x04,targetType,this); if changeItemType returns false then stop, else suspend -> read Item::getFrame, decrement/set frame, if frame>0 spawn helper and suspend to continue.

Type mapping is implemented with multiple explicit equality checks mapping specific type codes to their paired counterparts; success of FREE::changeItemType gates the continuation of the deactivate routine.
open_questions: Which exact in-game activator calls U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider (metadata suggests PLATE.func0411; this is inferred but not proven).
Are the listed type-code pairs exhaustive for all slider instances, or are there additional mappings not present in this snippet?
What visual effect or gameplay consequence does the spawned FREE::func1C19 produce (timer, particle, or visual frame helper is inferred but unknown)?
Does FREE::changeItemType sometimes fail due to collisions/occupied tile semantics, and are there higher-level consequences when deactivation aborts?

main_function.name: U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider
main_function.purpose: Animate and complete activation of a slider world object: play sound, advance frames until a terminal frame is reached, then replace the world item with its activated counterpart at the item's coordinates.
entry_conditions: Invoked by a triggering activator (inferred caller: PLATE.func0411 - e.g., a pressure plate or external trigger).
Runs when the slider item's frame is processed; repeatedly yields via suspend to implement multi-step animation.

function_map.name: U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider
function_map.role: Animate and finalize slider activation; swap world item to activated variant when terminal frame reached.

function_map.name: U8P_SLIDER::deactivateSlider
function_map.role: Reverse activation: attempt to revert world item to original type and step animation frames back to zero over time.

function_map.name: Item::*
function_map.role: Engine-provided item accessors used by both functions (getFrame/setFrame/getX/getY/getZ/getType/getQuantity/etc.).

function_map.name: FREE::changeItemType
function_map.role: Engine routine that performs world-tile/item replacement at given coordinates; success/failure influences deactivation flow.

function_map.name: spawn ->FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: Spawned helper/process used to pace or support multi-step animation (likely a short timer or effect).

external_calls.purpose: Play a sound effect to signal activation start (observed args: 0x64, 0x46).
Play a sound effect to signal deactivation start (same args observed).
Read the slider's current animation/frame index to decide whether to advance or finalize activation.
Write an incremented frame to progress the activation animation.
Read the slider's current frame so it can be decremented during deactivation.
Write the decremented frame value to continue the retreat animation.
Fetch X coordinate of the item to pass to FREE::changeItemType when finalizing activation.
Fetch Y coordinate of the item for changeItemType.
Fetch Z (level) coordinate of the item for changeItemType.
Fetch X coordinate to pass to FREE::changeItemType when reverting the world tile.
Fetch Y coordinate for changeItemType during deactivation.
Fetch Z coordinate for changeItemType during deactivation.
Determine which activated type code corresponds to the current item so the correct replacement type can be selected.
Detect the currently-placed activated type so the original type can be restored.
Replace the world item at (Z,Y,X) with an activated type once the terminal frame is reached.
Attempt to replace the world item with the deactivated/original type; deactivation proceeds only if this call succeeds.
Spawn a short-lived helper/process to pace or support the multi-step activation animation.
Spawn a short-lived helper/process during multi-step deactivation animation.
Yield the usecode/process to create time between animation frames.
Yield the usecode/process during deactivation animations.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_SLIDER::activateSlider animates a world "slider" item toward an activated state: it plays an SFX, reads the item's current frame, and either increments the frame (continuing a multi-step animation using spawn/suspend) or, when the frame reaches a terminal value (0x04), maps the item's type to an "activated" counterpart and calls FREE::changeItemType at the item's (Z,Y,X) to replace the world tile.
